comments.text: A weaker version of this conjecture has been proved by C. F. Lau in [La26]. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

Looks like the numerical simulation for the problem according to the OEIS sequence terminated quite early so with the help of Codex, I built a fast Rust scanner for this problem: https://github.com/conglu1997/erdos_413_computation Computations: - Scanned all barriers for ε=1 up to n = 100,000,000. - Found 200,211 barriers in this range. - Full list is in data/barriers_100m.txt (one n per line). - This extends OEIS A005236 well beyond the current table; should we submit an update? Density: - Empirical density at 10^8 is about 0.002. - Fitting the cumulative density to C / (log n)^α over the 100M dataset gives C ≈ 115.7, α ≈ 3.76 (see plots/density_fit.png). The decay looks consistent with that form over this range. Scripts to reproduce all included, and the saved data/plots are committed in the repo.

Also, seems like reducing ε greatly increases the number of barriers. For the same sweep up to 10^8, - ε = 1/2: - Barriers: 23,125,636 (density 0.2313) - ε = 1/3: - Barriers: 63,211,408 (density 0.6321) - ε = 1/4: - Barriers: 89,077,411 (density 0.8908) - ε = 1/5: - Barriers: 98,428,704 (density 0.9843)
